entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a plant uses water to produce biomass or yield

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of agricultural produce harvested per unit area

entities.name: PLANT PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The rate at which plants produce organic matter

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall leading to water shortage

entities.name: DRYLAND CEREALS
entities.description: Cereal crops grown in regions with limited rainfall

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Tropical regions characterized by low and irregular precipitation

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION DYNAMICS
entities.description: The temporal and spatial patterns of water loss through plant leaves

entities.name: MAXIMUM TRANSPIRATION RATE
entities.description: The highest possible rate of water loss through transpiration

entities.name: CROP FAILURE
entities.description: The total or partial loss of a crop due to adverse environmental conditions

entities.name: GRAIN FILLING
entities.description: The process of accumulating dry matter in the developing grain

entities.name: CROSS-ORGAN COORDINATION
entities.description: The synchronized functioning of different plant organs to regulate physiological processes

entities.name: AQUAPORINS
entities.description: Membrane proteins that act as channels for water transport

entities.name: PHENOLOGY
entities.description: The timing of biological events in a plant's life cycle

entities.name: RELATIVE ROOT AND SHOOT GROWTH
entities.description: The ratio of biomass allocation between the root system and the shoot system

entities.name: SHOOT ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The spatial arrangement and organization of the plant's above-ground parts

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH DISTRIBUTION PROFILE
entities.description: The spatial distribution of root length within the soil volume

entities.name: GRAVIMETRIC PHENOTYPING
entities.description: A method of measuring plant water use by weighing the soil-plant system

entities.name: BREEDING STRATEGY
entities.description: A planned approach to develop improved crop varieties through selection

entities.name: DIRECT SELECTION APPROACH
entities.description: A selection method focusing on the specific trait of interest rather than a proxy

entities.name: YIELD-BASED SELECTION
entities.description: A selection method based on the final yield of the plant

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in global temperatures and weather patterns

relationships.description: used as a solution to understand
